applicationInfo.name: e2e-ministack
applicationInfo.description:
### Overview

A minimal AWS CDK (TypeScript) stack — an S3 data bucket (with a server-access-log bucket) and a Node.js Lambda (`cdk-doubler`) with a customer-managed least-privilege role, KMS-encrypted log group, and an SQS dead-letter queue. The stack is exercised end-to-end against MiniStack (a local AWS emulator) in CI.

### Threat-modeling scope

Threats 1–4 are hand-authored and track the runtime resources in `lib/ministack-stack.ts`. Threats 5–12 (tagged `ai-generated`) cover the build/supply-chain and CI/CD surface (Docker socket, image/dependency/repo/workflow compromise, bootstrap bucket, DLQ disclosure, and the optional developer-invoked threat-composer-ai MCP server), then curated by hand. Each carries STRIDE + Priority metadata and assumption links so high-severity host-compromise threats are distinguishable from low-severity disclosure ones. Open in the [Threat Composer](https://github.com/awslabs/threat-composer) web app or the AWS Toolkit VS Code extension to expand further. See [docs/THREAT-MODELING.md](docs/THREAT-MODELING.md).

architecture.description:
### Components

- **DataBucket** (`cdk-demo-bucket`) — S3, SSE (S3-managed), TLS enforced, public access blocked, versioned, access logging to LogBucket.
- **LogBucket** (`cdk-demo-log-bucket`) — S3, receives server access logs.
- **Doubler** (`cdk-doubler`) — Node.js 24 Lambda; doubles `event.n`.
- **DoublerRole** — customer-managed IAM role, scoped write to the function's log group only.
- **DoublerLogGroup** — CloudWatch Logs, KMS-encrypted (`DoublerLogKey`), 1-week retention.
- **DoublerDlq** — SQS dead-letter queue (KMS-managed encryption, TLS enforced).

assumptions.content: Production deployments target a real AWS account; MiniStack is used only for local/CI testing and is not internet-exposed.
AWS account-level guardrails (SCPs, GuardDuty, CloudTrail) exist outside this stack and are out of scope for this model.

CI runs on ephemeral GitHub-hosted runners (ubuntu-latest), so host compromise is bounded to a single short-lived runner; workflow tokens are least-privilege (contents: read) and not persisted across jobs.

The threat-composer-ai MCP server (.mcp.json) is optional, developer-invoked, and never runs in CI; it executes on a developer workstation with that developer's AWS_PROFILE/Bedrock credentials and fetches third-party code via uvx pinned to a commit SHA.

mitigations.content: S3 buckets enforce TLS (`enforceSSL`), block all public access, and use SSE — preventing plaintext transport and public exposure.
The Lambda uses a customer-managed role granting only scoped CloudWatch Logs write to its own log group (no AWS-managed policy, no wildcard resources).
Failed asynchronous Lambda invocations are captured by an encrypted SQS dead-letter queue, preserving evidence and preventing silent data loss.
Lambda has a reserved concurrency limit, bounding blast radius and cost from a runaway or abusive invocation pattern.

Run MiniStack only on trusted local/CI hosts; never expose its endpoint or the Docker socket to untrusted networks. Consider a filtered Docker-socket proxy (e.g. tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy) instead of mounting the raw socket.
Pin the MiniStack image to a specific digest (not a floating tag) in CI; the image is third-party, so treat it as untrusted input and isolate the runner.
Lockfile-pinned deps with npm ci, npm audit + OSV-Scanner + Grype in CI, and a committed package-lock.json. Review dependency bumps in PRs.
Protect the default branch: require PR review, passing status checks (all security gates), and ideally signed commits. cdk-nag + checkov gate every change pre-merge.
Pin every GitHub Action to a commit SHA, set least-privilege top-level permissions (contents: read), and use persist-credentials: false. zizmor + actionlint audit the workflows in CI.
On a real account, restrict and monitor the CDKToolkit bootstrap bucket (block public access, enforce SSL, enable access logging and object-integrity checks); deploy only from trusted CI.
The DLQ uses KMS encryption and enforced TLS; restrict consume permissions and avoid logging sensitive payloads. Monitor DLQ depth with a CloudWatch alarm.

The threat-composer-ai MCP server is optional and pinned to a commit SHA in .mcp.json (not a floating ref); run it only on a trusted developer workstation, scope AWS_PROFILE to a least-privilege Bedrock-only profile, review the third-party code before invoking, and require explicit MCP-server approval in the client (/mcp in Claude Code). It is never wired into CI.

threats.threatAction: attempt to read or list objects in the data bucket
threats.statement: An internet-based actor without authentication can attempt to read or list objects in the data bucket, which leads to disclosure of stored data, resulting in reduced confidentiality of s3 data bucket

threats.threatAction: intercept S3 traffic sent over plaintext HTTP
threats.threatImpact: disclosure or tampering of objects in transit
threats.statement: An on-path actor in a man-in-the-middle position can intercept S3 traffic sent over plaintext HTTP, which leads to disclosure or tampering of objects in transit, resulting in reduced confidentiality and integrity of s3 data bucket

threats.threatSource: An actor who can influence the Lambda's input
threats.prerequisites: with the ability to invoke the function
threats.threatAction: send malformed or oversized payloads to the function
threats.threatImpact: unhandled errors or excessive resource consumption
threats.statement: An actor who can influence the Lambda's input with the ability to invoke the function can send malformed or oversized payloads to the function, which leads to unhandled errors or excessive resource consumption, resulting in reduced availability of cdk-doubler lambda

threats.threatSource: An actor with a foothold in the function's execution role
threats.threatAction: attempt to use the role's permissions to reach other AWS resources
threats.statement: An actor with a foothold in the function's execution role having compromised the function can attempt to use the role's permissions to reach other AWS resources, which leads to lateral movement beyond logging, resulting in reduced confidentiality and integrity of doubler iam role

threats.prerequisites: with access to the mounted Docker socket
threats.threatAction: escape the MiniStack container and run arbitrary commands on the host
threats.threatImpact: full host compromise including other containers, files, and credentials
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to the mounted Docker socket can escape the MiniStack container and run arbitrary commands on the host, which leads to full host compromise including other containers, files, and credentials, resulting in reduced confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the docker socket, host system, and ci environment

threats.prerequisites: who compromises the ministackorg/ministack image on Docker Hub
threats.threatAction: inject code that executes at container startup in CI and dev
threats.threatImpact: execution of arbitrary code with access to AWS credentials, source, and the Docker socket
threats.statement: A malicious actor who compromises the ministackorg/ministack image on Docker Hub can inject code that executes at container startup in CI and dev, which leads to execution of arbitrary code with access to AWS credentials, source, and the Docker socket, resulting in reduced confidentiality and integrity of the ministack container, ci environment, and source code

threats.prerequisites: who compromises an npm package in the dependency tree
threats.threatAction: execute code during npm install or at runtime
threats.threatImpact: theft of source, backdoors in CDK infrastructure, or credential exfiltration
threats.statement: A malicious actor who compromises an npm package in the dependency tree can execute code during npm install or at runtime, which leads to theft of source, backdoors in CDK infrastructure, or credential exfiltration, resulting in reduced confidentiality and integrity of npm dependencies, cdk infrastructure, and ci environment

threats.prerequisites: with commit access to the GitHub repository
threats.threatAction: inject malicious code into CDK stack definitions
threats.threatImpact: deployment of compromised infrastructure with persistent backdoors
threats.statement: A malicious actor with commit access to the GitHub repository can inject malicious code into CDK stack definitions, which leads to deployment of compromised infrastructure with persistent backdoors, resulting in reduced integrity and confidentiality of source code, cdk infrastructure, and iam policies

threats.prerequisites: who compromises a GitHub Actions workflow or action dependency
threats.threatAction: modify the CI pipeline to exfiltrate secrets or bypass security checks
threats.threatImpact: compromise of the CI environment and ability to modify deployed infrastructure
threats.statement: A malicious actor who compromises a GitHub Actions workflow or action dependency can modify the CI pipeline to exfiltrate secrets or bypass security checks, which leads to compromise of the CI environment and ability to modify deployed infrastructure, resulting in reduced confidentiality and integrity of github actions workflows, ci environment, and github secrets

threats.prerequisites: who compromises the CDKToolkit bootstrap bucket
threats.threatAction: replace CDK assets with malicious versions
threats.threatImpact: deployment of compromised Lambda code or other resources
threats.statement: A malicious actor who compromises the CDKToolkit bootstrap bucket can replace CDK assets with malicious versions, which leads to deployment of compromised Lambda code or other resources, resulting in reduced integrity of the cdktoolkit bootstrap bucket, lambda functions, and cdk assets

threats.prerequisites: who gains access to the SQS dead-letter queue
threats.threatImpact: disclosure of application data, error messages, or debugging information
threats.statement: A malicious actor who gains access to the SQS dead-letter queue can extract failed Lambda invocation payloads, which leads to disclosure of application data, error messages, or debugging information, resulting in reduced confidentiality of the sqs dead letter queue and lambda invocation payloads

threats.prerequisites: who tampers with the third-party threat-composer-ai code fetched by the developer-invoked MCP server (.mcp.json)
threats.threatAction: execute arbitrary code on the developer's workstation when the optional MCP server is run
threats.threatImpact: abuse of the developer's AWS_PROFILE/Bedrock credentials, exfiltration of local source or secrets, and tampering with the generated threat model
threats.statement: A malicious actor who tampers with the third-party threat-composer-ai code fetched by the developer-invoked MCP server (.mcp.json) can execute arbitrary code on the developer's workstation when the optional MCP server is run, which leads to abuse of the developer's AWS_PROFILE/Bedrock credentials, exfiltration of local source or secrets, and tampering with the generated threat model, resulting in reduced confidentiality and integrity of the developer workstation, developer aws credentials, and threat model artifact
